feed.title: Security Advisory for JavaScript packages hosted at npmjs.com
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for JavaScript packages hosted at npmjs.com on GitHub

entry.title: [@vitejs/plugin-rsc] Vite Plugin React has a Source Code Exposure Vulnerability in React Server Components
entry.content:
Impact

@vitejs/plugin-rsc vendors react-server-dom-webpack, which contained a vulnerability in versions prior to 19.2.3. See details in React repository's advisory https://github.com/facebook/react/security/advisories/GHSA-925w-6v3x-g4j4

Patches

Upgrade immediately to @vitejs/plugin-rsc@0.5.7 or later.

References

  • https://github.com/facebook/react/security/advisories/GHSA-925w-6v3x-g4j4
  • https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/security/advisories/GHSA-c6m7-q6pr-c64r
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-c6m7-q6pr-c64r

entry.title: [@vitejs/plugin-rsc] Vite Plugin React has a Denial of Service Vulnerability in React Server Components
entry.content:
Impact

@vitejs/plugin-rsc vendors react-server-dom-webpack, which contained a vulnerability in versions prior to 19.2.3. See details in React repository's advisory https://github.com/facebook/react/security/advisories/GHSA-7gmr-mq3h-m5h9

Patches

Upgrade immediately to @vitejs/plugin-rsc@0.5.7 or later.

References

  • https://github.com/vitejs/vite-plugin-react/security/advisories/GHSA-cpqf-f22c-r95x
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-cpqf-f22c-r95x

entry.title: [react-server-dom-webpack] Denial of Service Vulnerability in React Server Components
entry.content:
Impact

It was found that the fix to address CVE-2025-55184 in React Server Components was incomplete and does not prevent a denial of service attack in a specific case.

We recommend updating immediately.

The vulnerability exists in versions 19.0.2, 19.1.3, and 19.2.2 of:

  • react-server-dom-webpack
  • react-server-dom-parcel
  • react-server-dom-turbopack

These issues are present in the patches published on December 11th, 2025.

Patches

Fixes were back ported to versions 19.0.3, 19.1.4, and 19.2.3.

If you are using any of the above packages please upgrade to any of the fixed versions immediately.

If your app’s React code does not use a server, your app is not affected by this vulnerability. If your app does not use a framework, bundler, or bundler plugin that supports React Server Components, your app is not affected by this vulnerability.

References

See the blog post for more information and upgrade instructions.

References

  • https://github.com/facebook/react/security/advisories/GHSA-2m3v-v2m8-q956
  • https://github.com/facebook/react/security/advisories/GHSA-7gmr-mq3h-m5h9
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-67779
  • https://react.dev/blog/2025/12/11/denial-of-service-and-source-code-exposure-in-react-server-components
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7gmr-mq3h-m5h9

entry.title: [react-server-dom-webpack] Denial of Service Vulnerability in React Server Components
entry.content:
Impact

It was found that the fix to address CVE-2025-55184 in React Server Components was incomplete and does not prevent a denial of service attack in a specific case.

We recommend updating immediately.

The vulnerability exists in versions 19.0.2, 19.1.3, and 19.2.2 of:

  • react-server-dom-webpack
  • react-server-dom-parcel
  • react-server-dom-turbopack

These issues are present in the patches published on December 11th, 2025.

Fixes were back ported to versions 19.0.3, 19.1.4, and 19.2.3.

If you are using any of the above packages please upgrade to any of the fixed versions immediately.

If your app’s React code does not use a server, your app is not affected by this vulnerability. If your app does not use a framework, bundler, or bundler plugin that supports React Server Components, your app is not affected by this vulnerability.

  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7gmr-mq3h-m5h9

It was found that the fix to address CVE-2025-55184 in React Server Components was incomplete and does not prevent a denial of service attack in a specific case.

We recommend updating immediately.

The vulnerability exists in versions 19.0.2, 19.1.3, and 19.2.2 of:

  • react-server-dom-webpack
  • react-server-dom-parcel

If your app’s React code does not use a server, your app is not affected by this vulnerability. If your app does not use a framework, bundler, or bundler plugin that supports React Server Components, your app is not affected by this vulnerability.

entry.title: [react-server-dom-turbopack] Denial of Service Vulnerability in React Server Components
entry.content:
Impact

It was found that the fix to address CVE-2025-55184 in React Server Components was incomplete and does not prevent a denial of service attack in a specific case.

We recommend updating immediately.

The vulnerability exists in versions 19.0.2, 19.1.3, and 19.2.2 of:

  • react-server-dom-webpack
  • react-server-dom-parcel
  • react-server-dom-turbopack

These issues are present in the patches published on December 11th, 2025.

Patches

Fixes were back ported to versions 19.0.3, 19.1.4, and 19.2.3.

If you are using any of the above packages please upgrade to any of the fixed versions immediately.

If your app’s React code does not use a server, your app is not affected by this vulnerability. If your app does not use a framework, bundler, or bundler plugin that supports React Server Components, your app is not affected by this vulnerability.

See the blog post for more information and upgrade instructions.

Impact

It was found that the fix to address CVE-2025-55184 in React Server Components was incomplete and does not prevent a denial of service attack in a specific case.

We recommend updating immediately.

The vulnerability exists in versions 19.0.2, 19.1.3, and 19.2.2 of:

  • react-server-dom-webpack
  • react-server-dom-parcel
  • react-server-dom-turbopack

These issues are present in the patches published on December 11th, 2025.

Patches

Fixes were back ported to versions 19.0.3, 19.1.4, and 19.2.3.

If you are using any of the above packages please upgrade to any of the fixed versions immediately.

If your app’s React code does not use a server, your app is not affected by this vulnerability. If your app does not use a framework, bundler, or bundler plugin that supports React Server Components, your app is not affected by this vulnerability.

References

See the blog post for more information and upgrade instructions.

References

  • https://github.com/facebook/react/security/advisories/GHSA-7gmr-mq3h-m5h9
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-67779
  • https://react.dev/blog/2025/12/11/denial-of-service-and-source-code-exposure-in-react-server-components
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7gmr-mq3h-m5h9

entry.title: [next] Next Server Actions Source Code Exposure
entry.content:
A vulnerability affects certain React packages for versions 19.0.0, 19.0.1, 19.1.0, 19.1.1, 19.1.2, 19.2.0, and 19.2.1 and frameworks that use the affected packages, including Next.js 15.x and 16.x using the App Router. The issue is tracked upstream as CVE-2025-55183.

A malicious HTTP request can be crafted and sent to any App Router endpoint that can return the compiled source code of Server Functions. This could reveal business logic, but would not expose secrets unless they were hardcoded directly into Server Function code.

References

  • https://github.com/vercel/next.js/security/advisories/GHSA-w37m-7fhw-fmv9
  • https://nextjs.org/blog/security-update-2025-12-11
  • https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2025-55183
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w37m-7fhw-fmv9

entry.title: [next] Next Server Actions Source Code Exposure
entry.content:
A vulnerability affects certain React packages for versions 19.0.0, 19.0.1, 19.1.0, 19.1.1, 19.1.2, 19.2.0, and 19.2.1 and frameworks that use the affected packages, including Next.js 15.x and 16.x using the App Router. The issue is tracked upstream as CVE-2025-55183.

A malicious HTTP request can be crafted and sent to any App Router endpoint that can return the compiled source code of Server Functions. This could reveal business logic, but would not expose secrets unless they were hardcoded directly into Server Function code.

References

  • https://github.com/vercel/next.js/security/advisories/GHSA-w37m-7fhw-fmv9
  • https://nextjs.org/blog/security-update-2025-12-11
  • https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2025-55183
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w37m-7fhw-fmv9

A malicious HTTP request can be crafted and sent to any App Router endpoint that can return the compiled source code of Server Functions. This could reveal business logic, but would not expose secrets unless they were hardcoded directly into Server Function code.

entry.content:
A vulnerability affects certain React packages for versions 19.0.0, 19.0.1, 19.1.0, 19.1.1, 19.1.2, 19.2.0, and 19.2.1 and frameworks that use the affected packages, including Next.js 15.x and 16.x using the App Router. The issue is tracked upstream as CVE-2025-55183.

A malicious HTTP request can be crafted and sent to any App Router endpoint that can return the compiled source code of Server Functions. This could reveal business logic, but would not expose secrets unless they were hardcoded directly into Server Function code.
